summary: Matched gm_801B0DD0 in the gm_1B03 debug results screen path by replacing a local match-data typedef with struct DebugResultsData fields in gm_1B03.static.h, loading cached debug/sound-test match parameters into MatchEnd, accumulating character result SFX, and passing that result to lbAudioAx_8002702C. Bot report confirms one new match for main/melee/gm/gm_1B03::gm_801B0DD0.

key_files.role: Matched gm_801B0DD0 implementation; changed DebugResultsData access, MatchEnd setup, result SFX accumulation, and added PAD_STACK(8).
evidence_refs: pr_body: matched table lists gm_1B03.c gm_801B0DD0 ✓ 100%

key_files.role: Expanded struct DebugResultsData from an opaque pad into named bitfields, byte fields, padding, and MatchEnd match_end for gm_801B0DD0.
evidence_refs: diff: struct DebugResultsData pad_x0[0x2284] replaced with x0_0, x0_1, x0_other, x1-x4, pad_x5, match_end

what_changed: Removed the local GmB03MatchData typedef from gm_1B03.c and switched gm_801B0DD0 to use struct DebugResultsData from gm_1B03.static.h.
Converted DebugResultsData from a large opaque pad to explicit fields: x0_0, x0_1, x0_other, x1, x2, x3, x4, pad_x5, and MatchEnd match_end.
Changed un_803FA258 indexing from byte-offset-divided-by-4 forms such as 0x178 / 4 to direct element indexes such as 0x5E, 0x5F, and 0x60.
Added explicit casts for some gm_80166A98 player arguments and masks the first argument with 0xFF.
Accumulated lbAudioAx_80026E84 return values into a u64 sfx_result for eligible player standings and passed that value to lbAudioAx_8002702C instead of passing 0.
Added PAD_STACK(8) at the end of gm_801B0DD0.
PR body claims one matched function, gm_801B0DD0, and bot report confirms one new match with gm_801B0DD0 reaching 100.00%.

smart_moves.text: Moved recovered layout information into gm_1B03.static.h rather than keeping a function-local duplicate typedef, making the recovered DebugResultsData shape reusable in the translation unit.
evidence_refs: diff: removed typedef struct GmB03MatchData in gm_1B03.c
diff: struct DebugResultsData expanded in gm_1B03.static.h

smart_moves.text: Preserved the natural four-player loop and refined the loop body to accumulate audio result bits for non-CPU/non-big-loser standings.
Used typed MatchEnd access through match_end->player_standings instead of operating on opaque match-data storage.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a matched function's local scratch typedef overlaps with an existing static-header struct, moving the recovered fields into the owning static struct can improve type truthfulness while supporting a match.
Result-screen audio setup may require preserving the return value of per-character lbAudioAx_80026E84 calls and OR-ing them into a value passed to lbAudioAx_8002702C.

naming_conventions.name: match_end
naming_conventions.lesson: Use match_end for a MatchEnd* local derived from DebugResultsData::match_end.

naming_conventions.name: sfx_result
naming_conventions.lesson: Use sfx_result for the accumulated u64 result of lbAudioAx_80026E84 calls before lbAudioAx_8002702C.

naming_conventions.name: DebugResultsData fields
naming_conventions.lesson: This PR names the recovered DebugResultsData prefix fields as x0_0, x0_1, x0_other, x1, x2, x3, x4, pad_x5, and match_end; the semantic meaning of most prefix fields remains unknown.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use bitfields for individual flag bits in DebugResultsData instead of preserving manual masking assignments to a single u8 field.
evidence_refs: diff: old data->x0 mask assignments removed
diff: new u8 x0_0 : 1; u8 x0_1 : 1; u8 x0_other : 6

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Add a narrow PAD_STACK(8) only in the matched function after the functional calls; this is a matching tactic supported here by the bot's 100% match report but should not be generalized without verifier evidence.
evidence_refs: diff: PAD_STACK(8) added at end of gm_801B0DD0

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Keep the four-entry for loop for player standings rather than unrolling eligible-player audio handling.

follow_up_queries: un_803FA258 debug results sound test match parameters
DebugResultsData x0_0 x0_1 x0_other x1 x2 x3 x4

notes: No review comments were present in the loaded slice, so review_feedback is empty.

The PR body's functional description says gm_1B03.c is the debug results screen and describes loading cached match parameters and playing character-specific result fanfare; this is accepted only as PR evidence, not independently verified intent.
PAD_STACK(8) is supported by the diff plus bot match report for this function, but the slice does not include local objdiff output beyond the bot summary.
Many failed-function names in the PR body are search terms only; they were not changed in the diff and are not treated as lessons.

source_update_candidates.title: gm_1B03.c debug results screen role
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2489 describes gm_1B03.c as the debug results screen that loads cached match parameters from the sound test/debug menu into the results scene, rebuilds four-player standings, and plays character-specific results victory fanfare.
evidence_refs: pr_body: What these functions do section
source_update_candidates.reason: Could be useful as a path fact, but it comes from PR text rather than independent source analysis and should be curator-reviewed before promotion.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the listed 107 failed functions as changed or matched; they appear only in the PR body's failed-candidate list.
Do not generalize PAD_STACK(8) as a standard tactic from this PR alone; current standards require narrow verifier evidence for matching tactics.
Do not infer semantic meanings for x0_0, x0_1, x0_other, or x1-x4 beyond their names and assignments in the diff.
